top_findings.title: No SQLite / embedded DB — storage is a plain in-memory array
top_findings.evidence: src/database/Database.ts:17-95 stores books in a private BookRow[] (default DB_PATH=':memory:'); optional persistence is hand-rolled JSON via fs.writeFileSync (Database.ts:32-36). package.json dependencies list only express — no sqlite/better-sqlite3/sql.js. README states 'In-memory storage'.
top_findings.suggestion: Use better-sqlite3 (or sql.js) with a books table and parameterized queries, or at minimum a real embedded DB, per the task's technical constraint.

top_findings.title: Integration tests share a module-singleton DB with no per-test reset — count assertions are order-dependent
top_findings.evidence: tests/integration.test.ts uses the module-singleton db (src/database/Database.ts:95) but only clears require.cache once in beforeAll; there is no beforeEach/afterEach reset. By 'should list all books' an earlier POST test (line 40) has inserted a book, so expect(response.body.length).toBe(2) (line 114) and the author-filter count (line 135) fail. Consistent with test_coverage=0.1761.
top_findings.suggestion: Reset the store between tests (beforeEach re-import or db.clear()), or assert on relative counts / created ids rather than absolute lengths.
